office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: Incumbent Natasha Hill faces repeat challenger Tony Kiepe and independent John Kness, whose platform defers policy direction to voters.

candidates.summary: Kness is a candidate who says he would follow voters' direction rather than advance a detailed personal program.
highlights: Says he would follow voters' direction [S2]

experience.basis: The dossier documents no elected or institutional record.

candidates.summary: Hill is an incumbent representative and civil-rights attorney emphasizing tenant protections, health care, education, and civil rights.
highlights: Supports housing affordability and tenant protections [S2]
Supports health care, education, and civil rights [S2]

experience.basis: Hill is an incumbent representative with legal and advocacy experience.

candidates.summary: Kiepe is a health-insurance professional and repeat candidate focused on lower taxes, public safety, and treatment with accountability in homelessness policy.
highlights: Supports lower taxes and living costs [S2]
Supports treatment and accountability in homelessness policy [S2]

safety.basis: He combines treatment with accountability in homelessness policy.

experience.basis: He is a repeat candidate with professional experience but no documented elected record.
